posts.raw_content:
When once a republic has been corrupted, there is no possibility of remedying any of the growing evils but by removing the corruption and restoring its lost principles; every other correction is either useless or a new evil. 

This quote is often attributed to Thomas Jefferson, though it originates from Montesquieu's Spirit of the Laws, where Jefferson quoted it in his Commonplace Book.
